title: hieracium pilosum subsp. mollipellis
intro: Hieracium pilosum subsp. mollipellis, also known as the hairy hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The hairy hawkweed has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems, and is believed to have antimicrobial, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of Hieracium pilosum subsp. mollipellis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
